steps.title: Identify the interrupted structure
steps.content: The sentence says processing power has increased so drastically that dual-core technology is now everyday. The phrase “cores now number in the thousands” is parenthetical information inserted between paired dashes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — thousands—that: closes the parenthetical after “thousands” and lets “that” complete the main structure “so drastically ... that.” ✓
  • Choice B — thousands that—: puts “that” inside the parenthetical and leaves the main “so drastically ... that” structure incomplete. ✗
  • Choice C — thousands. That: creates a fragment because the “that” clause cannot stand alone. ✗
  • Choice D — thousands, that: uses a comma where the second dash is needed to close the parenthetical. ✗
That matches Choice A — thousands—that.
